steps: Nanodrop each isolated sample of RNA.
A good concentration of RNA is between 200 ng/µL - 2000 ng/µL . If the concentration is above 2000 ng/µL then dilute the sample with water for a final concentration below 2000 ng/µL .
A good 260/280 value is ~2.0.
A good 260/230 value is ~2.0-2.2.

cDNA for all brain regions is made with 2000 ng of RNA. cDNA for isolated cells is made with the highest amount of RNA that can be made from the least concentrated sample. TV per reaction = 20 µL ( 10 µL of RNA/Water + 10 µL of Master Mix) Calculate the RNA amount needed to make 2000 ng of RN for each sample Ex. For a RNA concentration of 1250.0 ng/µL . 2000/1250 = 1.6 µL of RNA
Calculate the amount of water to be added to the RNA for a TV = 10 µL Ex. For 1.6 µL of RNA 10 µL – 1.6 µL of RNA = 8.4 µL of Water

Thaw the isolated RNA and the following components of the High-Capacity cDNA reverse transcription kit On ice . 10X RT Buffer 25X dNTP Mix ( 100 millimolar (mM) ) 10X Random Primers – Can thaw at Room temperature RNase Inhibitor DO NOT thaw MultiScribe Reverse Transcriptase – it does not freeze at -20 °C and is prone to denaturing at higher temperatures. Keep at -20 °C until creating your master mix in go to step #7 below.
While the above components thaw On ice pipette the calculated amount of water (from go to step #3 of cDNA calculations) to PCR tubes. This step can be done at Room temperature .
Place the PCR tubes with water On ice and then add the calculated amount of RNA (from go to step #2 of cDNA calculations) to its respective PCR tube.
Create the following master mix On ice . Make enough master mix for each sample plus a little extra (if you have 10 samples, make enough master mix for 11).

Add reagents to a 1.5 mL tube in the following order. A B 1 Component Volume 2 1) Nuclease-free Water 3.2 µL 3 2)10X RT Buffer 2.0 µL 4 3)10X Random Primers 2.0 µL 5 4)RNase Inhibitor 1.0 µL 6 5)25X dNTP Mix 0.8 µL 7 6)MultiScribe Reverse Transcriptase 1.0 µL ***add last!! 8 TOTAL per reaction 10 µL
Mix gently.
Add 10 µL of master mix to each PCR tube On ice .
Mix PCR tubes gently then spin down briefly.
Keep On ice until performing the reverse transcription.
Place PCR tubes into the thermal cycler.
Set the Reaction Volume to 20 µL .
Set the following conditions:
Start the thermal cycler run.
When the samples reach go to step #14 take the PCR tubes out and store at 4 °C for short term use and at -20 °C for long term use.
